title: Vantara CEO Says Company Will Relocate Data Infrastructure to Europe
body: Nils Bergström announced Thursday that Vantara AI intends to migrate its core data processing infrastructure to data centers in Frankfurt and Amsterdam by the end of the year, a move he framed as a business decision rather than a response to regulatory pressure. "We were always going to build out European infrastructure as we grew," he told reporters at a Stockholm event. "The timing is coincidental." Most analysts read the announcement differently, noting that the company had made no previous public mention of European infrastructure plans and that the announcement came in the same week as a Parliamentary hearing in Brussels that highlighted Vantara by name. The company did not specify a vendor for the new infrastructure or an estimated cost. Its shares rose two percent on the news. A DataWatch EU representative said the migration, if complete, would address some but not all of the group's concerns, which also include the company's data retention and third-party sharing practices. The DPC did not comment.
